title: Add volume filters to system prune
body:
This change was missed in pull/8689. Now that volume pruneing supports
filters system pruneing can pass its filters down to the volume
pruneing. Additionally this change adds tests for the following components

* podman system prune subcommand with `--volumes` & `--filter` options
* apiv2 api tests for `/system/` and `/libpod/system` endpoints

Relates to #8453, #8672

Signed-off-by: Baron Lenardson lenardson.baron@gmail.com

comments.body: Nice work, I like the new tests, now you just need to get them to pass.  :^)

> Nice work, I like the new tests, now you just need to get them to pass. :^)

I'll have to dig into it more... it seems to pass on my machine
![confused](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15173997/102218129-24079480-3ea3-11eb-850d-07336e769d54.png)
The failures are on podman-remote.

@rhatdan / @Luap99

What is the status of refactoring the filter system? According to this comment: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/66e979a892df7dc820e6ce7ada0e3a34619c172f/cmd/podman/containers/prune.go#L67 there is some effort to refactor how filters are handled which would hugely benefit this PR as well as other filter related PRs. Currently I have a "passing" unit test that **should** fail as far as I can tell.

https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8724/files#diff-a65c0d6866cf06388a6c08d71af8d2bf8b130f89a4c3e9dafe45beced6064422R209-R213

The issue, as far as I can tell, is inconsistencies with how filter arguments are handled and passed to the underlying prune functions. I think refactoring sooner than later would greatly benefit podman and allow more consistent and testable code. For me just having a set of specific decisions would be nice. For example [system.go -> SystemPrune](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/66e979a892df7dc820e6ce7ada0e3a34619c172f/pkg/api/handlers/libpod/system.go#L16) in pkg/api/handlers/libpod/ prunes the Pod, Container, Image, and Volume components with prune functions that have 4 separate prototypes across 3 different packages. It would be nice if there was a more rigid pattern for this code path. I hope that all makes sense. I think if there were some clear guidelines such as "raw filter arguments must be parsed into a map[string][]string" or "subcommands that support filters must provide a `generateFilterFuncs`" then someone like myself could go through piece by piece and fix up components. I could take a stab at refactoring things however I am quite new to this project and am sure there is lots of background information and design considerations I am probably not aware of.
@bblenard No one is working on the refactor now, so if you want to take this on, go for it.

> @bblenard No one is working on the refactor now, so if you want to take this on, go for it.

I can try to take a poke at it now that I finally have this PR passing CI stuff.
@bblenard sadly you need a rebase.  Someone snuck in and touched some of the files you were working on.
need rebase first

@zhangguanzhang @Luap99 @giuseppe @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

> @zhangguanzhang @Luap99 @giuseppe @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL
> @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

For whoever else reviews this: I know libpod/filters/helpers.go is a full copy of pkg/domain/filters/helpers.go. I would like to move this code to the libpod/filters location as well as the pkg/domain/filters/volumes.go however I wanted to limit the scope of this PR.
@bblenard why do they need to be in libpod versus the pkg subdir?

> @bblenard why do they need to be in libpod versus the pkg subdir?

There is currently `pods.go` and `containers.go` in that subdirectory that provide `Generate...FilterFunc` style functions so I think `pkg/domain/filters/volumes.go` should also go there for consistency. That would leave just `helpers.go` in the `pkg/domain/filters` dir and when I wrote `ParseFilterArgumentsIntoFilters` I figured it was smart to keep it next to the other filter specific code however I wasn't aware of the libpod/filters code at that time.

That being said this is kinda along the lines of what I was talking about [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8724#issuecomment-745647515). If there is a better place for this code / `pkg` is a better spot for the code I'm fine putting it there, I just want to move towards a more consistent interface when it comes to filters

I'm honestly of the opposite opinion - this doesn't seem like `libpod` stuff to me, so I'd move it into `pkg/domain/filters`... But the goal of combining the two packages is entirely reasonable to me.
@mheon Cool, I figure I should still make that in its own PR yeah? Also what makes you say domain over libpod? I find I always struggle with this type of thing so I'm curious what you are thinking.

Generally speaking, Libpod does not include any CLI/frontend logic. I'll
make the argument that filters fit into the frontend category given that
they mostly affect what output is displayed (though it's honestly pretty
hazy in this case - can be argued either way).

Separate PR is definitely fine.
